annotations.label: Quantum chromodynamics

anchor.snippet: quantum chromodynamics ( QCD ) is the study of the strong interaction between quarks mediated by gluons

annotations.note: Mathlib has no physics formalization of QCD; greps for QCD/quark/gluon return nothing.

annotations.label: Color confinement

anchor.snippet: Due to the force between two color charges remaining constant as they are separated, the energy grows until a quark–antiquark pair is spontaneously produced

annotations.note: No QCD or confinement content exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Asymptotic freedom

anchor.snippet: a steady reduction in the strength of interactions between quarks and gluons as the energy scale of those interactions increases

annotations.note: Mathlib contains no renormalization-group or QCD coupling formalization.

annotations.label: Chiral symmetry breaking

anchor.snippet: the spontaneous symmetry breaking of an important global symmetry of quarks

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize chiral symmetry or spontaneous symmetry breaking in physics.

annotations.label: Color charge

anchor.snippet: The three kinds of charge in QCD (as opposed to one in quantum electrodynamics or QED) are usually referred to as " color charge "

annotations.note: No notion of color charge appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Confinement problem

anchor.snippet: Confinement : the equations of QCD remain unsolved at energy scales relevant for describing atomic nuclei

annotations.note: Mathlib has no QCD equations or confinement statement.

annotations.label: Quark matter

anchor.snippet: Quark matter : the equations of QCD predict that a plasma (or soup) of quarks and gluons should be formed at high temperature and density

annotations.note: Quark-gluon plasma is a physics concept absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Local symmetry

anchor.snippet: local symmetries , which are the symmetries that act independently at each point in spacetime

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of gauge/local spacetime symmetries.

annotations.label: Global symmetry

anchor.snippet: global symmetries , which are symmetries whose operations must be simultaneously applied to all points of spacetime

annotations.note: No physics notion of global symmetry exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: QCD as non-abelian gauge theory

anchor.snippet: QCD is a non-abelian gauge theory (or Yang–Mills theory ) of the SU(3) gauge group obtained by taking the color charge to define a local symmetry

annotations.note: Yang–Mills theory is not formalized in Mathlib; only the abstract group `specialUnitaryGroup` exists.

annotations.label: Approximate flavor symmetry

anchor.snippet: QCD has approximate flavor symmetry , which is broken by the differing masses of the quarks

annotations.note: Flavor symmetry is a physics concept absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chirality / handedness

anchor.snippet: If the spin of a particle has a positive projection on its direction of motion then it is called right-handed; otherwise, it is left-handed

annotations.note: Mathlib has no particle-physics notion of chirality/handedness.

annotations.label: Chiral symmetry

anchor.snippet: Chiral symmetries involve independent transformations of these two types of particle

annotations.note: Chiral symmetry of fermions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector (diagonal) symmetry

anchor.snippet: Vector symmetries (also called diagonal symmetries) mean the same transformation is applied on the two chiralities

annotations.note: No vector/diagonal chiral symmetry concept exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axial symmetry

anchor.snippet: Axial symmetries are those in which one transformation is applied on left-handed particles and the inverse on the right-handed particles

annotations.note: Axial symmetry of fermions is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Color group SU(3)

anchor.snippet: The color group SU(3) corresponds to the local symmetry whose gauging gives rise to QCD

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Matrix.specialUnitaryGroup n α` (so SU(3) is `specialUnitaryGroup (Fin 3) ℂ`), but not as a gauge group of QCD.

annotations.label: Global chiral flavor symmetry group

anchor.snippet: If one considers a version of QCD with N f flavors of massless quarks, then there is a global ( chiral ) flavor symmetry group

annotations.note: The chiral flavor symmetry group of QCD is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spontaneous chiral symmetry breaking by QCD vacuum

anchor.snippet: The chiral symmetry is spontaneously broken by the QCD vacuum to the vector (L+R)

annotations.note: Mathlib has no QCD vacuum or spontaneous symmetry breaking proposition.

annotations.label: Axial U(1) anomaly

anchor.snippet: The axial symmetry U A (1) is exact in the classical theory, but broken in the quantum theory, an occurrence called an anomaly

annotations.note: Quantum anomalies are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: QCD Lagrangian

anchor.snippet: The dynamics of the quarks and gluons are defined by the quantum chromodynamics Lagrangian

annotations.note: No Lagrangian field theory, let alone the QCD Lagrangian, is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gauge covariant derivative in QCD

anchor.snippet: the gauge covariant derivative [MATH] couples the quark field with a coupling strength

annotations.note: Mathlib has no gauge covariant derivative of physics.

annotations.label: Gluon field strength tensor

anchor.snippet: represents the gauge invariant gluon field strength tensor , analogous to the electromagnetic field strength tensor

annotations.note: No gauge field strength tensor is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wilson loop

anchor.snippet: An important theoretical concept is the Wilson loop (named after Kenneth G. Wilson )

annotations.note: Wilson loop is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quark fields

anchor.snippet: Quarks are represented by Dirac fields in the fundamental representation 3 of the gauge group SU(3)

annotations.note: Dirac fields and quark representations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gluon fields

anchor.snippet: Gluons are spin-1 bosons that also carry color charges , since they lie in the adjoint representation 8 of SU(3)

annotations.note: Gluon fields and SU(3) adjoint representation are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Antiquarks

anchor.snippet: Each type of quark has a corresponding antiquark, of which the charge is exactly opposite

annotations.note: No antiquark concept exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three basic QCD interactions

anchor.snippet: the above theory gives rise to three basic interactions: a quark may emit (or absorb) a gluon, a gluon may emit (or absorb) a gluon, and two gluons may directly interact

annotations.note: No QCD interaction vertices are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear confining potential

anchor.snippet: the effective potential between a quark and its anti-quark in a meson contains a term that increases in proportion to the distance between the quark and anti-quark

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize meson potentials or confinement.

annotations.label: Wilson loop area law

anchor.snippet: the above-mentioned stiffness is quantitatively related to the so-called "area law" behavior of the expectation value of the Wilson loop product

annotations.note: Wilson loop area law has no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Perturbative QCD

anchor.snippet: This approach is based on asymptotic freedom, which allows perturbation theory to be used accurately in experiments performed at very high energies

annotations.note: Perturbative QFT is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lattice QCD

anchor.snippet: This approach uses a discrete set of spacetime points (called the lattice) to reduce the analytically intractable path integrals of the continuum theory to a very difficult numerical computation

annotations.note: Lattice gauge theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1/N expansion

anchor.snippet: the 1 ⁄ N expansion , starts from the idea that the number of colors is infinite, and makes a series of corrections to account for the fact that it is not

annotations.note: Large-N expansion is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chiral perturbation theory

anchor.snippet: One such effective field theory is chiral perturbation theory or ChiPT, which is the QCD effective theory at low energies

annotations.note: ChiPT is a physics effective theory absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: QCD sum rules

anchor.snippet: Based on an Operator product expansion one can derive sets of relations that connect different observables with each other

annotations.note: Operator product expansion and QCD sum rules are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of glueballs

anchor.snippet: One qualitative prediction of QCD is that there exist composite particles made solely of gluons called glueballs

annotations.note: Glueballs are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mattis spin glasses analogy

anchor.snippet: the notion of gauge invariance forms the basis of the well-known Mattis spin glasses

annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain spin glass models.

annotations.label: Type-II superconductor analogy

anchor.snippet: There is also a correspondence between confinement in QCD – the fact that the color field is only different from zero in the interior of hadrons – and the behaviour of the usual magnetic field in the theory of type-II superconductors

annotations.note: Superconductivity models are absent from Mathlib.
